meaning: adj.多碎石的,碎石构成的,铺有碎石的
examples: Water runs through the gravelly soil very quickly.
There's a triumphant note in his gravelly voice.
The truck turned off the main road, and went along the gravelly track which led to the farm.
It was steeper here, and more thickly wooded, and the earth underfoot was gravelly and slick.
This may seem unremarkable, but what it really means is the soil is gravelly and complex.
